catalysts.catalyst_details: Modest potential risk to software sector estimates due to tariffs.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain positive on software, especially given recent pullback, while acknowledging modest potential risk to estimates from tariffs.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Potential risk to current CY25 guidance for software companies.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We explore two key themes: 1) Potential risk to current guidance (CY25)

catalysts.catalyst_details: Possibility of an 'air-pocket' or softening demand in the software sector as the year unfolds.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At best, Management teams will hold guidance unchanged near-term, although it’s possible we might enter an air-pocket as the year unfolds as we previously highlighted here.

catalysts.catalyst_details: CY25 average revenue growth for the software sector could see a 100-300 bps reduction.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This implies CY25 average revenue growth (GSe +13%) could see a 100–300 bps reduction (albeit -4% drawdown in USD since C1Q close vs. major currencies could be a partial mitigant).

catalysts.catalyst_details: A re-rating of the software sector to a normalized multiple (~8x EV/Sales NTM) could unlock meaningful upside into CY26.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At ~6x EV/Sales NTM, downside is modest if CY25 estimates are revised lower, while a re-rating to ~8x (normalized multiple) could unlock meaningful upside into CY26.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management teams are well-equipped to preserve FCF through cost agility, with Gen-AI investments unaffected.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Management teams remain well-equipped to preserve FCF through cost agility, with Gen-AI investments unaffected.

catalysts.catalyst_details: AI is seen as the software sector’s key catalyst, especially as focus moves from infrastructure/training CapEx to inferencing use cases at platform and application layers.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see AI as the sector’s key catalyst, especially as focus moves from infra/training CapEx to inferencing use cases at the platform and application layers.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Leading growth indicators (cRPO, NER, new logos) stabilized in the second half of 2024 after a multi-year deceleration.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe the worst is behind us for software fundamentals with leading growth indicators (cRPO, NER, new logos) stabilizing in C2H24 after a multi-year deceleration from peak levels.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Announcement of new tariffs on April 2nd, leading to uncertainty and potential impact on software purchasing behavior.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Following the April 2nd tariff announcement, we are following up on our initial Tariff Note to provide a range of Revenue/FCF scenarios (Exhibit 2) for our broader coverage to help investors better assess the potential knock-on effects to broader software purchasing behavior relative to the operating assumptions implicit in Management guidance exiting C4Q24.

catalysts.catalyst_details: C1Q results for the software sector are expected to be largely insulated from potential demand fallout associated with the tariff announcement, though April-close C1Q's may see modest impact.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Note that at a sector-level, we believe C1Q results will largely be insulated from any potential demand fallout associated with the tariff announcement (albeit April-close C1Q’s may observe a modest degree of impact on Revenue and cRPO)

catalysts.catalyst_details: Modest risk that management teams may provide more conservative guidance for C2Q and CY25 compared to Street expectations, due to tariff uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: but we acknowledge the modest risk potential that Management teams may opt to provide more conservative guidance on C2Q (vs. Street and implied guidance) and CY25.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management teams are likely to report deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes, and/or broader freezes in software procurement entering the June quarter (April/May).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: For subscription and consumption models alike, Management teams are likely to call out some level of deal cycle elongation, reduced contract sizes and/or broader freezes in software procurement entering the June quarter (April/May); at a minimum, they’re likely to acknowledge the risk of such a dynamic impacting upcoming quarters.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Revenue growth could be impacted by -70 to -280 bps assuming -5% to -20% cuts to net new revenue (NNR) due to tariff uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our net new revenue sensitivity analysis implies that average revenue growth for our coverage in CY25 (GSe: 13%) could come down -70 bps in a more conservative scenario of a -5% cut to NNR and upwards of -280 bps in a downside scenario where NNR is cut by -20% (Exhibit 2).

catalysts.catalyst_details: FCF growth can remain resilient even if a revenue slowdown scenario transpires, due to potential for FCFM expansion.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Revenue growth could be impacted by -70 to -280 bps assuming -5% to -20% cut to current NNR, albeit FCF growth can remain resilient if our slowdown scenario transpires.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The software sector is structurally more profitable as companies right-sized their cost structures following a post-COVID growth slowdown (CY22-24).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: One key reason is that the sector is structurally more proﬁtable as companies right-sized their cost structures following a post-COVID growth slowdown (CY22-24).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Analysis of net new revenue trends suggests potential for modest cuts if trends softened post-tariff announcement.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our analysis of net new revenue trends pre- and post-guidance points suggests potential for modest cuts if trends softened post-tariff announcement.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Management teams may incrementally de-risk expectations for 2Q and CY25 during the upcoming March/April quarter reporting season to factor for short-term demand fallout from tariff uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, we acknowledge the modest risk potential that Management teams incrementally de-risk expectations for 2Q and CY25 during the upcoming March/April quarter reporting season to better factor for any short-term demand fallout from tariff uncertainty.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Tariff uncertainty may cause management teams to revisit their assumptions around Net Expansion Rate (NER) and new logo acquisition if there is demand degradation in April.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: However, we don’t rule out the possibility that the impact of tariffs may cause Management teams to revisit their assumptions around NER and new logo acquisition if there were to be any demand degradation in the month of April.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Leading indicators for revenue growth were beginning to firm up in C2H24, with NER stabilizing and sequential customer growth inflecting modestly.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Leading indicators for revenue growth were beginning to ﬁrm up in C2H24, with NER stabilizing in the low-110’s (Exhibit 7) and sequential customer growth inﬂecting modestly to +3% (from a +2% trough in C1H24, Exhibit 6) across our coverage.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The knock-on effect of tariffs could cause enterprises to temporarily pause hiring plans, delay purchasing decisions, and pull back on transformational projects until tariff policies crystallize.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: On net, we anticipate that the knock-on effect of tariffs could cause many enterprises to temporarily pause hiring plans (impacting software seat growth), delay ongoing purchasing decisions, and/or generally pull back on more transformational projects until tariff policies crystallize.

catalysts.catalyst_details: cRPO growth rate and revenue coverage ratio will be important gauges of investors’ confidence in underwriting CY25 expectations.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: cRPO growth rate and revenue coverage ratio will be an important gauge of investors’ conﬁdence in underwriting CY25 expectations.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Software companies are entering CY25 with a slightly higher proportion of committed revenue (68% vs. 67% in C1Q24) relative to prior years, which could limit negative guidance revisions.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: While the environment has clearly changed, we note that companies in our coverage are entering CY25 with a slightly higher proportion of committed revenue vs. NTM guidance relative to the prior years (68% vs. 67% in C1Q24), which could help put a ceiling on the magnitude of potential negative guidance revisions (if any at all).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Software companies have variable cost leverage that can likely blunt the impact of any potential revenue growth slowdown, by adjusting operating costs.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Variable cost leverage can likely blunt the impact of any potential revenue growth slowdown.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Software companies are better equipped to navigate macro uncertainty due to structurally improved profitability from significant cost reduction initiatives post-COVID.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe software companies are better equipped to navigate through a potential growth slowdown relative to prior cycles considering most companies undertook signiﬁcant cost reduction initiatives post-COVID that translated to structurally improved margins, namely though workforce reductions, vendor consolidation and broader IT optimization.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Gen-AI is poised to drive the next leg of software growth despite short-term tariff-induced volatility.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Gen-AI is poised to drive the next leg of software growth despite short-term tariff-induced volatility.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Tariff disruptions, even if indirect, have the potential to accelerate AI adoption as enterprises seek productivity gains.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Tariff disruptions, even if indirect, have the potential to accelerate adoption as enterprises seek productivity gains.

catalysts.catalyst_details: 2025 may not be a major inflection point for Gen-AI due to budgetary constraints, but software companies are expected to foster engagement and adoption of productivity-driving AI products.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Albeit, this year may not mark a meaningful inﬂection point for Gen-Al given potential budgetary constraints against a more volatile macro backdrop, but we do expect many software companies to foster engagement and adoption of products that can drive real productivity against across the enterprise.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Stability in the macro environment and pervasive AI across workflows can pave the way for upside in software growth expectations in CY26.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, if we see stability in the macro environment and AI becomes more pervasive across workﬂows, we believe this can pave the way for upside for software growth expectations in CY26.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The emergence of a Gen-AI 'killer application' with meaningful utilization and monetization could become a catalyst for the software sector.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As such, we note that the market might be slow to participate in the Gen-AI theme until early signals of an ‘killer application’ with meaningful utilization and monetization emerges, which we then believe can become a catalyst for the sector.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Laying the groundwork in fostering AI adoption in 2025 is setting the foundation for a more robust value creation cycle and growth upside in 2026.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We acknowledge that while 2025 may not mark the breakout year for Gen AI or the emergence of a “killer app, ” we believe it is setting the foundation for a more robust value creation cycle in 2026.

catalysts.company: ServiceNow Inc., Salesforce Inc., Adobe Inc.
catalysts.catalyst_details: User conferences in late-Q2/Q3 (ServiceNow Knowledge, Salesforce Dreamforce, Adobe Max) could be a turning point for the Gen-AI narrative, providing greater visibility on AI adoption and revenue contribution.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As pricing models shift and feature sets mature throughout 2025, we believe user conferences in late-Q2/Q3 (such as ServiceNow Knowledge, Salesforce Dreamforce, Adobe Max) could be a turning point in the Gen-AI narrative, where we expect to gain greater visibility around AI adoption and revenue contribution.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The product innovation cycle in 2025 can pave the way to AI-driven value creation in 2026.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Product innovation cycle in 2025 can pave the way to AI-driven value creation in 2026.

catalysts.catalyst_details: AI-driven value creation will more meaningfully permeate in 2026 with a more contained inflationary environment, improved economic growth, and stability from tariff-induced volatility.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe AI-driven value creation will more meaningfully permeate in 2026 with a potentially more contained inﬂationary environment, improvement in economic growth, and stability from tariff-induced volatility.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Adobe is generating over $125M of AI-specific ARR and influencing more than $3.5B of AI-enabled bookings.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Tangible proof points already include Adobe generating over $125M of Al-speciﬁc ARR and inﬂuencing more than $3.5B of Al-enabled bookings;

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow saw Pro Plus deals more than quadruple year-over-year, including 39 deals with 3+ Now Assist products.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: ServiceNow saw Pro Plus deals more than quadruple yoy, including 39 deals with 3+ Now Assist products;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Salesforce is counting 3,000 paid AgentForce customers and roughly $900M of combined Data Cloud and AI ARR.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Salesforce counting 3,000 paid AgentForce customers and roughly $900M of combined Data Cloud and Al ARR;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Intuit is reporting a 20% higher ecosystem attach rate for AI-powered QuickBooks Live users.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Intuit reporting a 20% higher ecosystem attach rate for Al-powered QuickBooks Live users;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Microsoft is disclosing a $13B annualized run rate from AI services.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: and Microsoft disclosing a $13B annualized run rate from AI services;

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog is attributing 6% of both year-over-year growth (CY24) and total ARR to AI-native clients.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: and Datadog attributing 6% of both yoy growth (CY24) and total ARR to Al-native clients.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Twilio reported $260M in revenue from customers building in the AI space at its latest Investor Day.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Twilio also reported $260M in revenue from customers building in the AI space at its latest Investor Day.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Intrinsic Value Analysis indicates that most of the software coverage universe now trades at a discount.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We reiterate our Intrinsic Value Analysis, which indicates that most of our coverage universe now trades at a discount.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Current software valuations are well-below the zero-growth scenario intrinsic value, implying dislocation even with modest growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Furthermore, with our current coverage trading at valuation levels well-below our zero-growth scenario, we’d note that should even modest growth be accounted for, our coverage would still screen dislocated relative to its intrinsic value.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog is a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Datadog (12-month PT $127): We highlight Datadog in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of 26% top-line growth and 29% FCFM as of FY24.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog's strategic positioning as a leading end-to-end observability platform should enable it to sustain a growth rate of 20%+ for the foreseeable future.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We continue to believe that Datadog’s strategic positioning as a leading end-to-end observability platform should enable it to sustain a growth rate of 20%+ for the foreseeable future.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog's increasing contribution from AI revenue (6% of ARR in F4Q24 vs. 3% in F4Q23) is expected to continue outpacing core growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our conviction is further supported by the increasing contribution from AI revenue (comprising 6% of ARR in F4Q24 vs. 3% in F4Q23), which we believe can continue outpacing the growth of the ‘Core’.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Datadog shows healthy in-quarter metrics with NRR inflecting from mid-110s to high 110s in F4Q.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Coupled with healthy in-quarter metrics (NRR inﬂecting from mid-110’s to high 110’s in F4Q), we remain constructive on Datadog’s ability to scale into a $10bn business, support by secular tailwinds such as platform consolidation and DX / Gen-AI value garnering at the platform layer near-term as a robust expansion opportunity.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow is a high-quality asset with 20%+ long-term Subscription Revenue growth potential, a strong and expanding FCF margin profile (>30%), and a solid balance sheet.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: ServiceNow (12-month PT $1,150): We highlight ServiceNow as a high-quality asset with 20%+ long-term Subscription Revenue growth potential, a strong and expanding FCF margin proﬁle (>30%), and a solid balance sheet, meeting the key characteristics of a defensive software asset while still providing growth at-scale.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's broadly reiterated FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance of +19.5% CC (albeit -25 bps below prior) is underpinned by strong cRPO growth (+22% CC in F1Q) and healthy pipeline trends.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We see additional support from ServiceNow’s broadly reiterated FY25 Subscription Revenue guidance of +19.5% CC (albeit -25 bps below prior), underpinned by strong cRPO growth (+22% CC in F1Q) and healthy pipeline trends.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's current guidance appropriately contemplates risk from ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and potential Federal IT spending rationalization.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We also believe the current guidance appropriately contemplates risk from ongoing geopolitical uncertainty and potential Federal IT spending rationalization – Management is assuming ~ﬂat Fed NNACV from 2Q-4Q despite +30% PubSec NNACV growth in F1Q.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow stock is priced at an attractive entry point, being >20% off all-time highs and at ~36x EV/FCF CY26, despite the turbulent macro period.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: With NOW >20% off all-time highs (and at ~36x EV/FCF CY26), we believe the stock is priced at an attractive entry point, notwithstanding the turbulent macro period.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's standard for selling on customer value will enable it to continue gaining share of IT budgets against a potentially more constrained spending backdrop.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: ServiceNow remains the standard for selling on customer value, which we believe will enable the company to continue to gain share of IT budgets against a potentially more constrained spending backdrop, as in prior cycles.

catalysts.catalyst_details: ServiceNow's deeper foray into the CRM market, rollout of AI-enabled Pro Plus SKUs, and pending Moveworks acquisition will further augment its position.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: This is further complemented by ServiceNow’s deeper foray into the massive CRM market as well as through the rollout of its AI-enabled Pro Plus SKUs, which we believe can be further augmented by the company’s pending Moveworks acquisition.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Monday.com is a high-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, at a Rule of 60+ profile in FY24.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Monday.com (12-month PT $320): We highlight Monday.com in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with best-in-class unit economics, at a Rule of 60+ proﬁle in FY24, which we believe justiﬁes a premium vs. peers.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Monday.com's path to sustained high 20% growth is supported by newer products scaling (CRM, Dev, Service), bolstered Work Management capabilities driving wider deployments, and an increasing focus on AI products for customer retention and monetization.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain constructive on Monday’s path to becoming a strategic software provider and supporting sustained high 20% growth as: 1) Newer products scale (like CRM, Dev, and Service), 2) Bolstered Work Management capabilities drive wider deployments and support expansion upmarket, and 3) An increasing focus on AI products should support customer retention and eventual monetization.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Monday.com shows tangible momentum in AI usage (~10mm total AI actions), launched Monday Expert in March, and introduced consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight Monday’s tangible momentum in AI usage last quarter, with ~10mm total AI actions (vs. ~3mm/~1mm in Q3/Q2), Monday Expert launching in March, and the introduction of consumption-based pricing for AI Blocks (priced per successful request/block).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake is a high-quality asset with mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals and solidification of long-term growth strategy under current management.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Snowﬂake (12-month PT $205): We highlight Snowﬂake in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with an attractive combination of mid-20s topline growth and 25%+ FCFM, supported by stabilizing fundamentals and solidiﬁcation of long-term growth strategy under the current management.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake's core Data Warehousing/Engineering opportunity remains intact with NRR steadying at +126%, coupled with accelerating product velocity across Snowpark, Iceberg, and Cortex AI.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We highlight that the core Data Warehousing/Engineering opportunity remains intact with NRR steadying at +126% (vs. +127% in 2Q/3Q), coupled with product velocity accelerating across Snowpark, Iceberg, and Cortex AI.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake is expected to deliver OpM expansion following an AI investment cycle in CY25, coupled with a more efficient GTM motion, offering FCF support going forward.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: At the same time, we also see Snowﬂake delivering OpM expansion following an AI investment cycle in CY25 coupled with a more efﬁcient GTM motion, offering FCF support going forward.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Snowflake can become core to the development of AI applications at the platform layer and a long-term beneficiary of the Gen-AI cycle as spend permeates from Infrastructure into the Platform layer.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Looking ahead, we believe that as Snowﬂake can become core to the development of AI applications at the platform layer and a long-term beneﬁciary of the Gen-AI cycle as spend permeates from Infrastructure into the Platform layer.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlassian is a high-quality asset with durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin through FY27.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Atlassian (12-month PT $305): We highlight Atlassian in the current macro environment as a high-quality asset with durable 20%+ revenue growth and high-20s/low-30% FCF margin through FY27 .

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlassian is expected to execute on its 20% Cloud CAGR through FY27 and grow to become a $10bn business long-term.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: As Atlassian’s GTM business evolves and AI services continue to scale, we expect conviction to grow around Atlassian executing on its 20% Cloud CAGR through FY27 and, long term, for Atlassian to grow to become a $10bn business.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Atlassian has over 1 million monthly users of AI capabilities, growing customer enthusiasm, and competitive pricing, supporting its long-term positioning despite less near-term AI revenue upside.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In particular, with >1mn monthly users of AI capabilities and growing customer enthusiasm around adoption, the company’s competitive pricing leaves us increasingly positive around Atlassian’s long-term positioning, despite less near-term revenue upside from AI contribution.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab is a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to drive mid-20s growth and expanding FCF margins amid broader macro uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: GitLab (12-month PT $63): We highlight GitLab as a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to drive mid-20s growth and expanding FCF margins amid broader macro uncertainty.

catalysts.catalyst_details: The developer tool ecosystem is identified as first movers in the AI adoption cycle, with many tools already seeing strong adoption and capturing budget share.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: In particular, as we highlighted in our Private Software Company Conference note, we see the developer tool ecosystem as ﬁrst movers in the AI adoption cycle, noting many tools are already seeing strong adoption and capturing budget share.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab's strong competitive positioning, best-of-breed Gen-AI products, steadfast execution, and upmarket motion position the company well to execute on its growth opportunity.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe Gitlab’s strong competitive positioning, best-of-breed Gen-AI products, steadfast execution, and upmarket motion position the company well to execute on this opportunity.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab's refined focus on adding new paying customers (mid-market/enterprise), customer expansion, and pace of innovation provides multiple opportunities for long-term growth.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: More broadly, with GitLab’s focus reﬁned around 1) Adding new paying customers (particularly in the mid-market/enterprise), 2) Customer expansion, and 3) Pace of innovation, we believe there remain multiple opportunities to drive long-term growth.

catalysts.catalyst_details: GitLab is emerging as a clear leader in the fragmented DevOps market, driven by its full suite, ease-of-use, scalability, and focus on R&D and innovation, which can drive expanding wallet-share in a $40bn+ opportunity.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: GitLab is emerging as a clear leader in the fragmented DevOps market driven by its full suite covering the stages in the entire SDLC market, ease-of-use, scalability and focus on R&D and innovation, all of which can drive expanding wallet-share in a $40bn+ opportunity.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara is a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to deliver 30%+ topline growth amid broader macro uncertainty.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Samsara (12-month PT $46): We highlight Samsara as a best-in-class compounding growth asset that can continue to deliver 30%+ topline growth amid broader macro uncertainty.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara possesses significant competitive advantages in a large, underpenetrated TAM, while delivering consistent product innovation with operating leverage.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Our outlook is underpinned by Samsara’s signiﬁcant competitive advantages in a large, underpenetrated TAM while delivering consistent product innovation with operating leverage.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara shows expanding DBNRR across core (>115%) and large customers (>120%), growing contribution from emerging products (15% of NNACV), accelerating international growth (17% NNACV), and strength across key verticals (e.g., Construction).
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We are further encouraged by Samsara’s expanding DBNRR across the core at >115% and large customers at >120%, growing contribution from emerging products (contributing 15% of NNACV), accelerating international growth (contributing 17% NNACV), and strength across key verticals (e.g., Construction).

catalysts.catalyst_details: Samsara is evolving into a broader workflow application platform beyond its core telematics and driver safety products, expanding its footprint across a significantly larger asset management market.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Over time, we see Samsara evolving into a broader workﬂow application platform beyond its core telematics and driver safety products, expanding its footprint across a signiﬁcantly larger asset management market outside of core domains.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik is a best-in-class growth asset that can drive 20%+ ARR growth long-term with an elevated FCF margin expansion cadence.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Rubrik (12-month PT $82): We highlight Rubrik in the current macro environment as a best-in-class growth asset that can drive 20%+ ARR growth long-term with an elevated FCF margin expansion cadence.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik is uniquely suited to disrupt legacy competitors in the $11B+ Backup and Recovery market with its next-gen data security platform and expanding functionality.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We believe Rubrik is uniquely suited to continue to disrupt legacy competitors in the $11B+ Backup and Recovery market given its next-gen data security platform with ever-expanding functionality.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik's ongoing innovation velocity (extending workload coverage, launching new products like Identity Recovery, integrating DSPM within RSC) reaffirms its ability to expand its Cyber Resilience TAM.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: We remain compelled by Rubrik’s ongoing innovation velocity - extending workload coverage (re: Postgres, Red Hat OpenShift, OCI), launching new products such as Identity Recovery and natively integrating DSPM within RSC – reafﬁrming our view that Rubrik can expand its Cyber Resilience TAM.

catalysts.catalyst_details: Rubrik is well-positioned to execute due to its strong value proposition to enterprises, enabling CIOs to repurpose existing backup budgets for a next-generation data security platform with considerable TCO reduction.
catalysts.exact_sentences_in_the_file: Even in a constrained macro environment, we see Rubrik well-positioned to execute given the company’s already strong value proposition to the enterprise that enables CIOs to repurpose existing backup budgets for a next-generation data security platform that often comes with considerable TCO reduction vs. incumbent offerings.

file_Id: Americas Technology_ Software_ Navigating Turbulence Part VIII_ Mark-to-Market for Potential Tariff Impact.pdf
